newsSinceJun6: EGM notice dispatched for June 22, 2026 meeting: enabling approval to divest Inox Green Energy Services stake from 51.13% to <=50% (in tranches), plus director remuneration items. This is optionality for subsidiary monetization/deconsolidation, not a signed transaction. The weak Q4 FY26 print (-9% day move, brokerage target cuts) and the offsetting FY27 guidance (~75% revenue growth, ~3.1 GW order book, >24 months revenue visibility) predate June 6 and were reconciled in yesterday's verdict. Today's +1.9% is a modest bounce near the 52-week low (Rs 75.05 vs Rs 86.14), below the 5% threshold.

sources.label: ScanX - Inox Wind EGM notice: director remuneration and Inox Green stake-sale enabling approval
Business Standard - Inox Wind slips 9% on weak Q4, brokerages cut targets (June 1, background)
Power Peak Digest - Inox Wind weak Q4 but guides 75% FY27 revenue growth, ~3.1 GW order book

verdicts.rankRationale: On the scaling (capacity build-out) lens, the thesis is intact: ~3.1 GW order book and 75% FY27 revenue-growth guidance support the wind-equipment build-out case, and the post-Q4 sell-off was already reconciled in yesterday's hold. The only genuinely new item since June 6 - the EGM enabling resolution to take Inox Green below 50% - is mild balance-sheet optionality, not enough to re-rank; small positive drift only.
verdicts.bull: Stock sits near its 52-week low with the order book and FY27 ramp guidance intact, so the Q4-driven derating has improved asymmetry; Inox Green deconsolidation could unlock cash and clean up the structure ahead of execution-heavy FY27.
verdicts.neutral: Q4 was weak and broker targets were cut, but guidance and the 3.1 GW backlog keep the capacity build-out thesis alive; the EGM item is enabling-only, so nothing changes the rank case yet.
verdicts.bear: Q4 miss signals execution slippage just as the FY27 guidance bar was set very high (75% growth); if order-to-execution conversion stalls again, the backlog optics won't protect the stock, and a stake sale below 50% could be read as cash-strain rather than optionality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 22 EGM approval and subsequent Inox Green stake-sale tranches, plus Q1 FY27 execution evidence on the 3.1 GW backlog.
verdicts.primaryRisk: FY27 execution miss against the 75% revenue-growth guidance after a weak Q4, triggering further broker downgrades.
